title: pediomelum esculentum
intro: Pediomelum esculentum, also known as the Indian breadroot or esculent breadroot, is a perennial plant native to North America. The plant's roots contain inulin, a type of polysaccharide with potential medicinal properties. Traditionally, the roots of Pediomelum esculentum have been used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems and skin conditions. The plant can be prepared in herbal forms such as teas, capsules, and extracts to harness its potential health benefits.
